---
title: "Matching in R Practicum"
author: "Alexander Dean"
subtitle: "Recitation Period 6"
institute: "Vanderbilt University"
email: "alexander.r.dean@vanderbilt.edu"
date: today
format: 
  revealjs:
    theme: dark
    footer: "Alexander Dean (Vanderbilt University)"
    slide-number: true
    embed-resources: true
    link-external-icon: true
    link-external-newwindow: true
    self-contained-math: true
    incremental: false
    self-contained: true
    citations-hover: true
    fragments: true
    scrollable: false
    transition: fade
    reference-location: document
fontsize: 32px
editor: source
---

## What We Will Cover Today

1. Implementing matching methods in $\texttt{R}$

## A Research Question:

RQ: How does being asked to pay a bribe to a government employee affect your trust of the state?

. . . 

Hypotheses:
    - $H_0$: Being asked to pay a bribe to a government employee does not affect your trust in the state
    - $H_1$: Being asked to pay a bribe to a government employee does affect your trust in the state

## The Data:

1. Americas Barometer 2023 survey wave from CGD
2. IV: Self-report question on being asked to pay a bribe
3. DV: Composite trust index with various state agencies
4. Covariates: age, gender, education, income, follows the news frequency, news from social media frequency, and importance of religion

## Additional Resources

1. [Regularly updated list](https://christinecai.github.io/PublicGoods/applied_micro_methods.pdf) of new methodological advancements in applied statistics. Very helpful when starting a paper with an unfamiliar method
2. [cobalt reference manual](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/cobalt/cobalt.pdf)
3. [Matching reference manual](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/Matching/Matching.pdf)
4. [MatchIt reference manual](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/MatchIt/MatchIt.pdf)
5. [Getting started with MatchIt](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/MatchIt/vignettes/MatchIt.html#matching)
